summary: Audience‑recorded tape with muddy sound but a legendary Box of Rain moment makes this Hampton show unforgettable
review: Fans rave about the rare and emotional Box of Rain performance that turned an otherwise ordinary March 1986 Hampton show into a treasured memory, even though the recording is plagued by distant, crowd‑overwhelming audio. The first set is described as mediocre with a flubbed Althea, while the second set lifts the vibe, but listeners should expect muddy, low‑clarity sound typical of an audience tape.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is rated high because of the standout Box of Rain and overall fan excitement; recording quality is consistently described as poor, pulling the weighted average down to a solid but not stellar rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Very loud crowd dominates the mix; sound is muddy and distant, with low clarity and limited instrument separation. Listeners report it feels like a crowd recording with faint music in the background.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set described as mediocre and even featured a lyrical flub on Althea; second set ignited the audience, highlighted by Box of Rain and upbeat dancing, giving the night its lasting emotional impact despite the sound issues.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Flubbed lyrics on Althea; some reviewers note his performance was off that night.
